entry.title: Password Expiry is a Terrible Policy
entry.summary:
A pet peeve, and an opinion piece:

Enforced password expiry is a terrible policy which encourages users to choose a minimally secure password. Given a set of password requirements (number of characters, content requirements, etc.) the user will almost certainly choose a password which only just meets those password requirements …
entry.content:
A pet peeve, and an opinion piece:

Enforced password expiry is a terrible policy which encourages users to choose a minimally secure password. Given a set of password requirements (number of characters, content requirements, etc.) the user will almost certainly choose a password which only just meets those password requirements, if they know they will need to change their password after some period of time. Why bother creating and memorizing a good, secure password, if I need to change it in 90 days?

A better solution is to encourage password complexity, and to impliment proper password content and quality settings. Require your users to use OWASP compliant passwords, encourage them to go beyond OWASP requirements, and ditch password expiry. Of course, the proper solution is to implement multi-factor authentication which should obviate the need to expire passwords altogether.
